page.content: In the Bechtel Group's FY2023 report on Infrastructure spending allocations, the category breakdown reveals Roads with an allocation of $50M, a P/E ratio of 1.5, a current ratio of 1.2, liquidity metrics at 200%, leverage ratios of 60%, gross margin of 15%, net profit margin of 8%, and a return on assets of 5%. Bridges received $30M, with a P/E ratio of 1.8, current ratio of 1.1, liquidity metrics at 150%, leverage ratios of 55%, gross margin of 12%, net profit margin of 7%, and return on assets of 6%. Railways were allocated $80M, accompanied by a P/E ratio of 2.0, current ratio of 1.4, liquidity metrics at 160%, leverage ratios of 65%, gross margin of 18%, net profit margin of 9%, and return on assets of 4%. Utilities saw $40M, featuring a P/E ratio of 1.2, current ratio of 1.0, liquidity metrics at 120%, leverage ratios of 62%, gross margin of 10%, net profit margin of 6%, and return on assets of 3%.

page.content: The total allocation sums to $270M, with an overall P/E ratio of 1.6, current ratio of 1.2, liquidity metrics at 150%, leverage ratios of 59%, gross margin of 13%, net profit margin of 7%, return on assets of 4%, and projected growth of 10%.

lines.text: The total allocation sums to $270M, with an overall P/E ratio of 1.6, current ratio of 1.2, liquidity metrics at 150%, leverage
ratios of 59%, gross margin of 13%, net profit margin of 7%, return on assets of 4%, and projected growth of 10%.

page.content:
The fiscal year is 2023, the region is North America, with the currency in USD, indicating overall trends of increasing investment, sector growth, and sustainable practices. 

Regarding material costs, Steel has a cost index of $120.5 with a change of 8.3%, Concrete at $115.2 with a change of 5.1%, Wood at $110.3 with a change of 4.0%, Brick at $130.1 with a change of 9.7%, Glass at $125.4 with a change of 7.2%, and Aluminum at $135.6 with a change of 10.5%.
